text.title: Video Generation Services and Generation Types
text.summary: Service comparison table, generation type matrix, and provider selection guidance for AI video generation.

list.item: Runway Gen-3 Alpha Turbo: Very High quality, 5-10s clips, 1080p, official Python SDK, API available
Luma Dream Machine: High quality, 5s clips, 720p, REST API (no official SDK), URL expires ~1h
Pika 2.0: High quality, 4s clips, 1080p, partial API support, strong for image-to-video
Replicate (SVD, AnimateDiff, Zeroscope): Good quality, 4-16 frames, open models, cheapest, lowest quality floor
OpenAI Sora: Highest quality, 60s clips, 1080p, invite-only as of Q2 2026, no public API
Kling: Very High quality, 5s clips, 1080p, REST API, latency varies (Chinese service)
Text-to-Video: text prompt → video clip. Lowest consistency control.
Image-to-Video: image + prompt → video animating the image. Use when subject consistency matters.
Video-to-Video: existing video + prompt → modified video. Limited availability.
Video Extension: final frame of existing clip → new segment. Luma keyframe API supports this natively.

rule.statement: Use image-to-video instead of text-to-video whenever subject or scene consistency matters across shots.
rule.rationale: Text-to-video generates subjects from scratch each time — faces, objects, and lighting are inconsistent across separate generations.

example.code: PROVIDER_MAP = { "photorealistic": "luma", # strong motion, photorealism "cinematic": "runway", # best structured API, commercial quality "animated": "replicate", # AnimateDiff for stylized/anime "prototype": "replicate", # cheapest for testing prompts } # Always generate 3+ variations per shot — selection is cheaper than re-prompting
